title: Signal Tanker Weekly Report
summary:
Chart of the Week: VLCC AG-FE Supply - Demand
Number of ships at Ras Tanura port of loading increases as demand ton-miles drops to FE
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform- Supply / Demand Ton Charts
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-reports-historicaltl/2nxx2t/318438471?h=iE-9c3fddzTNyk9GXeEOH8rtwBgseygv3M9OZzjfK0M
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/tanker-reports-toncharts-trade/2nxx2x/318438471?h=iE-9c3fddzTNyk9GXeEOH8rtwBgseygv3M9OZzjfK0M
Freight rates for VLCC crude oil tankers continued to weaken in the second week of May, with the number of vessels at the Ras Tanura port of loading rising to a surprising level (left chart above) and reaching a high of more than 100 while ton-miles demanded from AG to China for VLCC tankers is at a lower level than the same period last year. In the oil market, the economic recession and rising oil inventories in the U.S. Oilprice.com reported that Brent crude traded down 1.86% on the day to $76.00 per barrel, with a loss of $1.44, while West Texas Intermediate traded down 2.13% on the day to $72.14 per barrel, with a loss of $1.57.

Freight rates for VLCC crude oil tankers continued to weaken in the second week of May, with the number of vessels at the Ras Tanura port of loading rising to a surprising level (left chart above) and reaching a high of more than 100 while ton-miles demanded from AG to China for VLCC tankers is at a lower level than the same period last year. In the oil market, the economic recession and rising oil inventories in the U.S.

children.title: ‘ Dirty ' VLCC - Weaker | Suezmax - Aframax - Firmer
section_path: ‘ Dirty ' VLCC - Weaker | Suezmax - Aframax - Firmer
children.section_path_text: ‘ Dirty ' VLCC - Weaker | Suezmax - Aframax - Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-12T114949.429.png | assets/2023-05-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-12t114949429_431c9b5ba92f.png
Sentiment on VLCC crude oil freight rates weakened in the second week of May, with the Aframax Med route experiencing an upward correction in the last three weeks. VLCC AG-FE freight rates were at WS42, nearly 30 points below the recent peak in week 16.

children.title: ‘ Product ' LR2 - Firmer | LR1 - Softening
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-12T115039.146.png | assets/2023-05-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-12t115039146_918659e672b3.png
LR2 AG freight rates hovered around WS 160, 10 points higher than the previous week. LR1 MEG -to-Japan freight rates eased to WS 180, 20 points lower than the previous week.

children.title: Product ' Panamax - Weaker
children.summary: Panamax Carib-to-USG rates have weakened further since late April. Rates are now at WS 190, down 50% from the recent peak in weeks 12 and 13.

children.title: ‘ Clean ' MR2 - MR1 - Weaker
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-12T115129.738.png | assets/2023-05-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-12t115129738_d3e709a228de.png
MR1 rates Algeria-to-Med rates remained at WS150, almost 50% lower than mid-April, and Baltic-to-Cont MR1 rates fell to ~ WS140, 20 points lower than the previous week. MR2 rates Cont-to-US are now at WS125, indicating decreasing trend dynamics, down nearly 60% from early April.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY - (# vessels) 'Dirty' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas VLCC Increasing | Aframax - Suezmax Decreasing
section_path: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY - (# vessels) 'Dirty' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas VLCC Increasing | Aframax - Suezmax Decreasing
children.section_path_text: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY - (# vessels) 'Dirty' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas VLCC Increasing | Aframax - Suezmax Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-12T115213.728.png | assets/2023-05-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-12t115213728_e40250084a14.png
The supply of VLCC crude oil tankers showed an upward trend in the first half of May, while the other crude oil tanker classes showed a downward trend. VLCC Ras Tanura : The number of vessels is now 81, 7 more than the annual average.

children.title: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | LR2 | Increasing
section_path: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | LR2 | Increasing
children.section_path_text: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | LR2 | Increasing

children.title: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | MR1 | Decreasing
section_path: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | MR1 | Decreasing
children.section_path_text: ‘ Clean ' Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas | MR1 | Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-12T115324.599.png | assets/2023-05-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-12t115324599_9676e866a019.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The number of vessels continues to increase and tends to fall below the annual average of 14 after three consecutive weeks of increase. Clean MR1 Algeria Skikda: The number of vessels decreased to 38, 12 less than the previous week, with a trend of further decrease in the third week of May.

children.title: ‘ Dirty ' | VLCC - Decreasing | Suezmax - Aframax - Increasing
section_path: ‘ Dirty ' | VLCC - Decreasing | Suezmax - Aframax - Increasing
children.section_path_text: ‘ Dirty ' | VLCC - Decreasing | Suezmax - Aframax - Increasing

children.title: ‘ Clean ' | Panamax - MR2 - MR1 - Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-05-12T125612.929.png | assets/2023-05-12_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-05-12t125612929_7cf821ec492f.png
Dirty demand ton-days: VLCC demand continued to weaken, while the Suezmax and Aframax segments continued to experience an upswing. Clean demand ton-days / Panamax demand: The previous week's high was eroded by a sharp downward correction in the second week of May, and there is uncertainty about a recovery in the coming days.
